title: Alectorolophus subulatus
taxonomy: Alectorolophus subulatus is a species of flowering plant in the family Aizoaceae. It is native to the southwestern part of the Western Cape province in South Africa. The plant is often classified as succulent due to its water-storing ability. Within the family Aizoaceae, Alectorolophus subulatus is further classified into the subfamily Ruschioideae. Further research may be necessary to identify the specific genus and subgenus classification of the plant.

intro: Alectorolophus subulatus, also known as Chinese angelica or dong quai, is a perennial plant belonging to the Apiaceae family, commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine. The health benefits of Alectorolophus subulatus include its ability to regulate menstrual cycles, reduce menopausal symptoms, and improve cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of this plant include ferulic acid, essential oils, and triterpenoid saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Alectorolophus subulatus is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, as well as in combination with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine formulas.
